slides.title: When you sip coffee *shapes your sleep*.
slides.sub: Caffeine lingers far longer than the buzz. An earlier cutoff can protect your deepest sleep.

slides.title: A long half-life
slides.body: Caffeine fades slowly. Half of it *can still linger* in your body many hours after the cup.

slides.title: Afternoon coffee stays
slides.body: That 3pm cup *may still be working* at bedtime, even if you feel ready to sleep.

slides.title: It trims deep sleep
slides.body: Caffeine late in the day *is linked to* lighter, less restorative sleep. You may not even notice.

slides.title: Set a daily cutoff
slides.body: Try keeping coffee to the morning and early afternoon. An earlier last cup *can help* your nights.

slides.title: Enjoy it, time it
slides.body: You do not have to quit. Simply moving your last cup earlier *may protect* deeper sleep.

slides.title: Protect the sleep that *restores you*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food and movement and turns those habits into one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your coffee timing matters more than you might think. 🌿

Caffeine has a long half-life, so an afternoon cup can quietly trim your deep sleep. An earlier cutoff is an easy fix. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep and daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Your Coffee Timing Affects Deep Sleep
